intro_script: "Welcome to the Daily Droid, your source of up-to-date news and insights on the rapidly evolving world of robotics. Today, July 24th, 2026, we've got six stories shaping the future of automation and artificial intelligence that you won't want to miss."
outro_script: "Thanks for gear-grinding with us today, July 24, 2026! That's a huge boost for ATOMS - talk about batteries powering innovation! Until tomorrow, when we'll be recharging the conversation, say goodbye and keep on robot-ing!"

items.title: Uber co-founder raises $1.7B for new robotics startup ATOMS
items.summary: Former Uber co-founder and CEO Travis Kalanick has raised $1.7 billion in equity investment for his new robotics startup ATOMS, with funding from top investors including a16z, Ben Horowitz, Bain Capital, and others. The funds will enable ATOMS to complete its "bits-to-atoms" story arc, focusing on building specialized robots for various industrial sectors such as food production, mining, and mobile robots. Lead investor a16z is joining the company's board alongside other prominent partners.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Uber co-founder raises $1.7B for new robotics startup ATOMS," Uber co-founder and CEO Travis Kalanick has secured $1.7 billion in equity investment for his new robotics startup ATOMS. This significant funding will enable the company to complete its "bits-to-atoms" story arc, focusing on building specialized robots for various industrial sectors such as food production, mining, and mobile robots. Top investors including a16z, Ben Horowitz, Bain Capital, have contributed to this massive funding round. Lead investor a16z is joining the company's board alongside other prominent partners, marking a significant boost to ATOMS' growth prospects. With this substantial investment, ATOMS aims to further accelerate its development of advanced robotics solutions for various industries.

items.title: Holiday Robotics raises $105M for its FRIDAY wheeled humanoid
items.summary: Holiday Robotics raised $105 million in Series A funding to build a wheeled humanoid robot called FRIDAY, designed to work safely alongside people in manufacturing environments. The robot features a wheel-based mobile base, dexterous hands with tactile sensing and force-aware control, and advanced perception systems. Holiday aims to increase reliability through its vision-language-skill approach, which combines hardware, simulation, learning, and deployment into a full-stack system.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Holiday Robotics raises $105M for its FRIDAY wheeled humanoid." Holiday Robotics has secured $105 million in Series A funding to further develop and deploy its wheeled humanoid robot, dubbed FRIDAY. This significant investment will enable the company to enhance its vision-language-skill approach, a full-stack system that integrates hardware, simulation, learning, and deployment, with the goal of increasing reliability. By combining these elements, Holiday Robotics aims to create a robot that can safely work alongside humans in manufacturing environments. The FRIDAY robot boasts a wheel-based mobile base, dexterous hands equipped with tactile sensing and force-aware control, and advanced perception systems. This funding will support the company's efforts to deploy this technology and expand its capabilities.

items.title: AMD unveils Kria module for real-time control, unified memory for robots
items.summary: AMD has unveiled the Kria module for real-time control and unified memory for robots, designed to deliver compute and AI workload processing for physical AI. The Ryzen AI Embedded X100 series and Kria AI Robotics platform offer deterministic real-time control, unified CPU-GPU-NPU memory, and an open software stack that can outperform NVIDIA's architecture on system-level robotics workloads.
items.audio_script:
From The Robot Report, "AMD unveils Kria module for real-time control, unified memory for robots," AMD has unveiled a new module designed for real-time control and unified memory in robots. The Ryzen AI Embedded X100 series and Kria AI Robotics platform offer deterministic real-time control, as well as unified CPU-GPU-NPU memory that can be shared across the system, providing a potentially more efficient and scalable solution for robotics workloads.

The AMD team has developed a custom SoC designed to deliver high-performance compute and AI workload processing in physical robots. The Kria module is built on an open software stack, allowing developers to create their own applications and integrations. Compared to NVIDIA's architecture, the Ryzen AI Embedded X100 series can outperform on system-level robotics workloads, making it a compelling alternative for companies looking to build robots with advanced AI capabilities.

This development marks a significant step forward for robotics, potentially opening up new possibilities for industries such as manufacturing, healthcare, and logistics. As AMD continues to refine its offerings in the AI space, investors and industry professionals should watch closely for future updates on this platform, which is expected to have a significant impact on the robotics landscape.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Convert in the terminal
items.summary: A free CLI tool that can convert CAD assemblies to URDF without requiring any CAD plugins is being considered for open-sourcing. The proposed tool would allow users to work directly with ROS from their terminal with just one command. Participants were asked if they would use such a tool, but also inquired about the biggest reason why they might prefer existing CAD add-ins instead.
items.audio_script:
From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Convert in the terminal," a free CLI tool that can convert CAD assemblies to URDF without requiring any CAD plugins is being considered for open-sourcing. This development could enable users to work directly with ROS from their terminal using just one command, streamlining the conversion process and providing a more seamless integration between CAD software and ROS.

Researchers have been exploring this concept, examining how such a tool would be implemented and tested, as well as engaging with potential users through surveys. Participants were asked if they would use a CLI-based conversion tool, with responses indicating that some users see benefits in using a command-line interface for such tasks. However, others expressed concerns about the limitations of a text-based approach, citing existing CAD add-ins as an alternative.

The proposed open-sourcing of this tool could have significant implications for the robotics and AI industries, offering a more accessible entry point for developers and hobbyists alike. As the project moves forward, users can expect to see updates on the development progress and potential release dates.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Logging and Observability Guide Review Part 2 | Cloud Robotics WG Meeting 2026-08-24
items.summary: There was a Cloud Robotics WG Meeting on August 24, 2026, from 4:00-5:00 PM UTC. The meeting continued editing the first draft of the Logging and Observability guide, which had been started in the previous session but not recorded due to lack of engaging video content. The next meeting link is available for sign-up or notification reminders through Google Group or calendar notifications.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Logging and Observability Guide Review Part 2 | Cloud Robotics WG Meeting 2026-08-24". The Cloud Robotics Working Group continued refining the first draft of its Logging and Observability guide during a meeting on August 24, 2026. After the previous session's unrecorded meeting due to lack of engaging video content, the group resumed editing the document, with contributions from participants shaping the final version. The updated guide is now being reviewed, with an emphasis on clarity and consistency in logging best practices for cloud robotics applications. The review process will ensure that the document accurately reflects industry standards and best practices.

items.title: A mini robot to simplify dental treatment
items.summary: A miniature robot, called "MIR" (Miniature Intraoral Robot), has been developed at the University of Basel to simplify dental treatment by precisely preparing teeth for a crown. The robot measures 43x26x28mm and can be fitted comfortably into an open mouth, reducing the need for multiple appointments with dentists. Researchers have tested the robot's precision, finding it to be less than 0.2 millimeters in positional error, which will be further reduced after sensors are integrated into the system. The next step is to integrate sensors and a camera to enable monitoring of its position and progress during treatment.
items.audio_script: From Robohub, "A mini robot to simplify dental treatment" is a story about researchers at the University of Basel developing a miniature robot called "MIR" that precisely prepares teeth for a crown by measuring 43x26x28mm and fitting comfortably into an open mouth. The MIR has demonstrated impressive precision in its current form, with positional error measured at less than 0.2 millimeters, a result set to be further improved upon once sensors are integrated into the system. Researchers have conducted extensive testing on the robot's capabilities, showcasing its effectiveness in streamlining dental treatment procedures and reducing the need for multiple appointments with dentists. The next step involves integrating a camera and monitoring equipment to enable real-time tracking of the robot's position and progress during treatment, paving the way for enhanced accuracy and efficiency in dental care.

items.title: HERMES: Heterogeneous Edge-Relational Multi-Head Embedded SSM Attention for Traffic Conflict Prediction at Signalized Intersections
items.summary: HERMES, a heterogeneous edge-relational graph neural network with SSM-informed multi-head attention, was proposed for traffic conflict prediction at signalized intersections. Vehicles and pedestrians were represented as heterogeneous nodes, while vehicle-vehicle, vehicle-pedestrian, and pedestrian-pedestrian interactions were encoded as relation-specific edges. HERMES achieved an AUC-ROC of 0.9898, outperforming the strongest Transformer baseline and XGBoost, with a detection rate of 95.7% at a 5% false-alarm rate.

items.title: PhysCoRe: Physics-Corrected Residual World Models for Material-Aware Deformable Dynamics
items.summary: PhysCoRe presents a physics-corrected residual world model that combines a Material Point Method (MPM) simulator with two feed-forward neural networks. The design includes a material refinement module, which infers per-particle elasticity from visual observations, and a residual correction module, which learns the discrepancy between the simulator's internal dynamics and predicted corrections. This approach supports online material identification on novel objects and outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in prediction accuracy, providing a reliable distribution of confidence across an object's geometry. PhysCoRe achieves this by adapting its predictive uncertainty to steer further exploration toward regions where its estimate is least confident.

items.title: Scalable Low-Cost Laboratory Automation: A Digital Twin-Integrated Robotic Platform for Autonomous Liquid Handling (RAINBOTTM)
items.summary: A low-cost, openly reproducible liquid-handling robot called RAINBOTTM was developed by converting a consumer-grade Cartesian 3D printer. The robot features a precision single-channel pipette actuated through the printer's X--Y--Z gantry, with plunger and tip-eject motions controlled by Python. A browser-based digital twin synchronises with the physical platform in real-time, allowing remote monitoring and intervention, and is coupled to the CEID framework for goal-directed inverse-design search. The complete hardware costs under US$1300 establish an accessible physical--virtual framework for self-driving laboratory automation.

items.title: Safe and Scalable Multi-Drone Payload Transport via CBF-based Reinforcement Learning with Zero-Shot Sim-to-Real Transfer
items.summary: Researchers have developed a learning-based framework for safe and scalable multi-drone cooperative payload transport using Discrete Graph Control Barrier Function Proximal Policy Optimization (DGPPO). The framework introduces a minimal 2D abstraction that preserves task-relevant drone-payload coupling while remaining computationally efficient. A single learned policy generalizes across varying team sizes and task scenarios, and demonstrates safe operation in dynamic environments with other drone teams acting as obstacles.

items.title: Towards Capability-Aware Traversability Navigation for Unstructured Environments
items.summary: Researchers propose Capability-Aware Traversability (CAT), a framework embedding physical limits into spatial feature space. CAT grounds dense supervision masks in physical trajectories through an interactive annotation pipeline and modulates semantic terrain maps with robot-specific traversability vectors using Spatially-Adaptive Denormalization (SPADE) blocks. On human-annotated and trajectory-aligned datasets, CAT leads all ranking-based metrics, improving AUROC by 11.0% and AUPRC by 15.8%.

items.title: FELT: Generating Tactile Signals from Vision for Visuo-Tactile Manipulation
items.summary: FELT, a learning-based framework, synthesizes per-finger pressure tactile images from RGB observations, reducing the need for tactile-equipped data collection. FELT uses a frozen visual encoder and a lightweight query decoder to predict tactile signals in a single feed-forward pass, capturing asymmetric contact patterns during interactions. At inference time, FELT only requires RGB data, allowing existing vision-only data to be augmented with tactile observations as generated tactile images or latent tactile features.

items.title: Decentralized UAV Swarms for Ground Target Protection in GPS- and Communication-Denied Environments
items.summary: Researchers propose a decentralized UAV swarm system to protect against ground target attacks in GPS- and communication-denied environments. The swarm uses onboard sensors, Kalman filters, and relative measurements to track targets and coordinate as a unit. The approach is validated with real robots, demonstrating its effectiveness in detecting, encircling, and intercepting hostile UAVs.

items.title: Self-Supervised Bio-Inspired Robotic Trajectory Planning with Obstacle Avoidance
items.summary: Researchers Miroslav Krupa, Miroslav Cibula, and Kristína Malinovská have developed a self-supervised bio-inspired robotic trajectory planning framework with obstacle avoidance using forward and inverse models. The approach demonstrates feasibility but exploits the learning signal provided by the models, requiring additional training regimes and mitigation strategies to improve generalization.

items.title: A real-time RGB-D perception pipeline for autonomous impact hammers in mining: self-filtering, rock segmentation and rock-breaking poses generation
items.summary: A real-time RGB-D perception pipeline for autonomous impact hammers in mining simultaneously generates operationally feasible rock-breaking poses and a robot-free 3D representation of the workspace. The proposed approach combines image-based instance segmentation with geometric point cloud processing, operating on embedded hardware at approximately 10 Hz with a total latency of around 675 ms. This enables responsive closed-loop behavior when integrated with a control system. Experimental results in a representative scaled scenario demonstrate that the proposed system is suitable for real-time autonomous impact hammer operation.
